              differentiable=group[\"differentiable\"],\n   193→                capturable=group[\"capturable\"],\n   194→                has_complex=has_complex,\n   195→            )\n   196→\n   197→        return loss\n   198→\n   199→\n   200→RMSprop.__doc__ = (\n   201→    r\"\"\"Implements RMSprop algorithm.\n   202→\n   203→    .. math::\n   204→       \\begin{aligned}\n   205→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   206→            &\\textbf{input}      : \\alpha \\text{ (alpha)}, \\: \\gamma \\text{ (lr)},\n   207→                \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)}                   \\\\\n   208→            &\\hspace{13mm}   \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)},\\: \\mu \\text{ (momentum)},\n   209→                \\: centered, \\: \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}                                       \\\\\n   210→            &\\textbf{initialize} : v_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (square average)}, \\:\n   211→                \\textbf{b}_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ (buffer)}, \\: g^{ave}_0 \\leftarrow 0     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   212→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   213→            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n   214→            &\\hspace{5mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})           \\\\\n   215→            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                                    \\\\\n   216→            &\\hspace{10mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda  \\theta_{t-1}                            \\\\\n   217→            &\\hspace{5mm}v_t           \\leftarrow   \\alpha v_{t-1} + (1 - \\alpha) g^2_t\n   218→                \\hspace{8mm}                                                                     \\\\\n   219→            &\\hspace{5mm} \\tilde{v_t} \\leftarrow v_t                                             \\\\\n   220→            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: centered                                                          \\\\\n   221→            &\\hspace{10mm} g^{ave}_t \\leftarrow g^{ave}_{t-1} \\alpha + (1-\\alpha) g_t            \\\\\n   222→            &\\hspace{10mm} \\tilde{v_t} \\leftarrow \\tilde{v_t} -  \\big(g^{ave}_{t} \\big)^2        \\\\\n   223→            &\\hspace{5mm}if \\: \\mu > 0                                                           \\\\\n   224→            &\\hspace{10mm} \\textbf{b}_t\\leftarrow \\mu \\textbf{b}_{t-1} +\n   225→                g_t/ \\big(\\sqrt{\\tilde{v_t}} +  \\epsilon \\big)                                   \\\\\n   226→            &\\hspace{10mm} \\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma \\textbf{b}_t                \\\\\n   227→            &\\hspace{5mm} else                                                                   \\\\\n   228→            &\\hspace{10mm}\\theta_t      \\leftarrow   \\theta_{t-1} -\n   229→                \\gamma  g_t/ \\big(\\sqrt{\\tilde{v_t}} + \\epsilon \\big)  \\hspace{3mm}              \\\\\n   230→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   231→            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   232→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   233→       \\end{aligned}\n   234→\n   235→    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to\n   236→    `lecture notes <https://www.cs.toronto.edu/~tijmen/csc321/slides/lecture_slides_lec6.pdf>`_ by G. Hinton.\n   237→    and centered version `Generating Sequences\n   238→    With Recurrent Neural Networks <https://arxiv.org/pdf/1308.0850v5.pdf>`_.\n   239→    The implementation here takes the square root of the gradient average before\n   240→    adding epsilon (note that TensorFlow interchanges these two operations). The effective\n   241→    learning rate is thus :math:`\\gamma/(\\sqrt{v} + \\epsilon)` where :math:`\\gamma`\n   242→    is the scheduled learning rate and :math:`v` is the weighted moving average\n   243→    of the squared gradient.\n   244→    \"\"\"\n   245→    + rf\"\"\"\n   246→    Args:\n   247→        {_params_doc}\n   248→        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-2)\n   249→        alpha (float, optional): smoothing constant (default: 0.99)\n   250→        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n   251→            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n   252→        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n   253→        momentum (float, optional): momentum factor (default: 0)\n   254→        centered (bool, optional) : if ``True``, compute the centered RMSProp,\n   255→            the gradient is normalized by an estimation of its variance\n   256→        {_capturable_doc}\n   257→        {_foreach_doc}\n   258→        {_maximize_doc}\n   259→        {_differentiable_doc}\n   260→\n   261→    \"\"\"\n   262→)\n   263→\n   264→\n   265→def _single_tensor_rmsprop(\n   266→    params: list[Tensor],\n   267→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   268→    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   269→    grad_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   270→    momentum_buffer_list: list[Tensor],\n   271→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   272→    *,\n   273→    lr: float,\n   274→    alpha: float,\n   275→    eps: float,\n   276→    weight_decay: float,\n   277→    momentum: float,\n   278→    centered: bool,\n   279→    maximize: bool,\n   280→    differentiable: bool,\n   281→    capturable: bool,\n   282→    has_complex: bool,\n   283→):\n   284→    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   285→        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   286→\n   287→    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n   288→        step = state_steps[i]\n   289→\n   290→        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   291→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   292→            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n   293→            assert (\n   294→                param.device.type == step.device.type\n   295→                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   296→            ), (\n   297→                f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   298→            )\n   299→\n   300→        grad = grads[i]\n   301→        grad = grad if not maximize else -grad\n   302→        square_avg = square_avgs[i]\n   303→\n   304→        step += 1\n   305→\n   306→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   307→            grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   308→\n   309→        is_complex_param = torch.is_complex(param)\n   310→        if is_complex_param:\n   311→            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n   312→            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n   313→            square_avg = torch.view_as_real(square_avg)\n   314→\n   315→        square_avg.mul_(alpha).addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1 - alpha)\n   316→\n   317→        if centered:\n   318→            grad_avg = grad_avgs[i]\n   319→            if is_complex_param:\n   320→                grad_avg = torch.view_as_real(grad_avg)\n   321→            grad_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - alpha)\n   322→            avg = square_avg.addcmul(grad_avg, grad_avg, value=-1).sqrt_()\n   323→        else:\n   324→            avg = square_avg.sqrt()\n   325→\n   326→        if differentiable:\n   327→            avg = avg.add(eps)\n   328→        else:\n   329→            avg = avg.add_(eps)\n   330→\n   331→        if momentum > 0:\n   332→            buf = momentum_buffer_list[i]\n   333→            if is_complex_param:\n   334→                buf = torch.view_as_real(buf)\n   335→            buf.mul_(momentum).addcdiv_(grad, avg)\n   336→            param.add_(buf, alpha=-lr)\n   337→        else:\n   338→            param.addcdiv_(grad, avg, value=-lr)\n   339→\n   340→\n   341→def _multi_tensor_rmsprop(\n   342→    params: list[Tensor],\n   343→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   344→    square_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   345→    grad_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   346→    momentum_buffer_list: list[Tensor],\n   347→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   348→    *,\n   349→    lr: float,\n   350→    alpha: float,\n   351→    eps: float,\n   352→    weight_decay: float,\n   353→    momentum: float,\n   354→    centered: bool,\n   355→    maximize: bool,\n   356→    differentiable: bool,\n   357→    capturable: bool,\n   358→    has_complex: bool,\n   359→):\n   360→    if len(params) == 0:\n   361→        return\n   362→\n   363→    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n   364→\n   365→    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   366→    if not torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   410→\n   411→        # Update steps\n   412→        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   413→        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   414→        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   415→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   416→            torch._foreach_add_(\n   417→                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   418→            )\n   419→        else:\n   420→            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n   421→\n   422→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   423→            # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) already allocated for maximize\n   424→            if maximize:\n   425→                torch._foreach_add_(grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay)\n   426→            else:\n   427→                grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   428→                    grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n   429→                )\n   430→\n   431→        torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_square_avgs, alpha)\n   432→        torch._foreach_addcmul_(\n   433→            grouped_square_avgs, grouped_grads, grouped_grads, value=1 - alpha\n   434→        )\n   435→\n   436→        if centered:\n   437→            grouped_grad_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grad_avgs_)\n   438→            torch._foreach_lerp_(grouped_grad_avgs, grouped_grads, 1 - alpha)\n   439→            avg = torch._foreach_addcmul(\n   440→                grouped_square_avgs, grouped_grad_avgs, grouped_grad_avgs, value=-1\n   441→            )\n   442→            torch._foreach_sqrt_(avg)\n   443→            torch._foreach_add_(avg, eps)\n   444→        else:\n   445→            avg = torch._foreach_sqrt(grouped_square_avgs)\n   446→            torch._foreach_add_(avg, eps)\n   447→\n   448→        if momentum > 0:\n   449→            grouped_momentum_buffer_list = cast(\n   450→                list[Tensor], grouped_momentum_buffer_list_\n   451→            )\n   452→            torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_momentum_buffer_list, momentum)\n   453→            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_momentum_buffer_list, grouped_grads, avg)\n   454→            # closure()\n   141→\n   142→        for group in self.param_groups:\n   143→            params_with_grad: list[Tensor] = []\n   144→            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n   145→            exp_avgs:   149→\n   150→            has_complex = self._init_group(\n   151→                group, params_with_grad, grads, exp_avgs, exp_avg_sqs, state_steps\n   152→            )\n   153→\n   154→            radam(\n   155→                params_with_grad,\n   156→                grads,\n   157→                exp_avgs,\n   158→                exp_avg_sqs,\n   159→                state_steps,\n   160→                beta1=beta1,\n   161→                beta2=beta2,\n   162→                lr=group[\"lr\"],\n   163→                weight_decay=group[\"weight_decay\"],\n   164→                eps=group[\"eps\"],\n   165→                maximize=group[\"maximize\"],\n   166→                foreach=group[\"foreach\"],\n   167→                capturable=group[\"capturable\"],\n   168→                differentiable=group[\"differentiable\"],\n   169→                decoupled_weight_decay=group[\"decoupled_weight_decay\"],\n   170→                has_complex=has_complex,\n   171→            )\n   172→\n   173→        return loss\n   174→\n   175→\n   176→RAdam.__doc__ = (\n   177→    r\"\"\"Implements RAdam algorithm.\n   178→\n   179→    .. math::\n   180→       \\begin{aligned}\n   181→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   182→            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\beta_1, \\beta_2\n   183→                \\text{ (betas)}, \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\:f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)}, \\:\n   184→                \\lambda \\text{ (weightdecay)}, \\:\\textit{maximize}                               \\\\\n   185→            &\\hspace{13mm} \\epsilon \\text{ (epsilon)}, \\textit{decoupled\\_weight\\_decay}         \\\\\n   186→            &\\textbf{initialize} :  m_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( first moment)},\n   187→                v_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( second moment)},                                       \\\\\n   188→            &\\hspace{18mm} \\rho_{\\infty} \\leftarrow 2/(1-\\beta_2) -1                      \\\\[-1.ex]\n   189→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}  \\\\\n   190→            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n   191→            &\\hspace{6mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\n   192→            &\\hspace{12mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   -\\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})         \\\\\n   193→            &\\hspace{6mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n   194→            &\\hspace{12mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})          \\\\\n   195→            &\\hspace{6mm} \\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1}                                       \\\\\n   196→            &\\hspace{6mm} \\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                          \\\\\n   197→            &\\hspace{12mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{decoupled\\_weight\\_decay}                       \\\\\n   198→            &\\hspace{18mm} \\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t} - \\gamma \\lambda \\theta_{t}            \\\\\n   199→            &\\hspace{12mm}\\textbf{else}                                                          \\\\\n   200→            &\\hspace{18mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda \\theta_{t}                               \\\\\n   201→            &\\hspace{6mm}m_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_1 m_{t-1} + (1 - \\beta_1) g_t          \\\\\n   202→            &\\hspace{6mm}v_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_2 v_{t-1} + (1-\\beta_2) g^2_t          \\\\\n   203→            &\\hspace{6mm}\\widehat{m_t} \\leftarrow   m_t/\\big(1-\\beta_1^t \\big)                   \\\\\n   204→            &\\hspace{6mm}\\rho_t \\leftarrow \\rho_{\\infty} -\n   205→                2 t \\beta^t_2 /\\big(1-\\beta_2^t \\big)                                    \\\\[0.1.ex]\n   206→            &\\hspace{6mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\rho_t > 5                                               \\\\\n   207→            &\\hspace{12mm} l_t \\leftarrow \\frac{\\sqrt{ (1-\\beta^t_2) }}{ \\sqrt{v_t} +\\epsilon  } \\\\\n   208→            &\\hspace{12mm} r_t \\leftarrow\n   209→      \\sqrt{\\frac{(\\rho_t-4)(\\rho_t-2)\\rho_{\\infty}}{(\\rho_{\\infty}-4)(\\rho_{\\infty}-2) \\rho_t}} \\\\\n   210→            &\\hspace{12mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_t - \\gamma \\widehat{m_t} r_t l_t        \\\\\n   211→            &\\hspace{6mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n   212→            &\\hspace{12mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_t - \\gamma \\widehat{m_t}                \\\\\n   213→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   214→            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   215→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   216→       \\end{aligned}\n   217→\n   218→    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `On the variance of the adaptive learning rate and beyond`_.\n   219→\n   220→    This implementation provides an option to use either the original weight_decay implementation as in Adam\n   221→    (where the weight_decay is applied to the gradient) or the one from AdamW (where weight_decay is applied\n   222→    to the weight) through the decoupled_weight_decay option. When decoupled_weight_decay is set to False\n   223→    (default), it uses the original Adam style weight decay, otherwise, it uses the AdamW style which\n   224→    corresponds more closely to the `author's implementation`_ in the RAdam paper. Further information\n   225→    about decoupled weight decay can be found in `Decoupled Weight Decay Regularization`_.\n   226→\n   227→    \"\"\"\n   228→    + rf\"\"\"\n   229→    Args:\n   230→        {_params_doc}\n   231→        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 1e-3)\n   232→        betas (Tuple[float, float], optional): coefficients used for computing\n   233→            running averages of gradient and its square (default: (0.9, 0.999))\n   234→        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n   235→            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n   236→        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n   237→        decoupled_weight_decay (bool, optional): whether to decouple the weight\n   238→            decay as in AdamW to obtain RAdamW. If True, the algorithm does not\n   239→            accumulate weight decay in the momentum nor variance. (default: False)\n   240→        {_foreach_doc}\n   241→        {_maximize_doc}\n   242→        {_capturable_doc}\n   243→        {_differentiable_doc}\n   244→\n   245→    .. _On the variance of the adaptive learning rate and beyond:\n   246→        https://arxiv.org/abs/1908.03265\n   247→    .. _author's implementation:\n   248→        https://github.com/LiyuanLucasLiu/RAdam\n   249→    .. _Decoupled Weight Decay Regularization:\n   250→        https://arxiv.org/abs/1711.05101\n   251→\n   252→    \"\"\"\n   253→)\n   254→\n   255→\n   256→def _single_tensor_radam(\n   257→    params: list[Tensor],\n   258→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   259→    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   260→    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   261→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   262→    *,\n   263→    beta1: float,\n   264→    beta2: float,\n   265→    lr: float,\n   266→    weight_decay: float,\n   267→    eps: float,\n   268→    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n   269→    differentiable: bool,\n   270→    maximize: bool,\n   271→    capturable: bool,\n   272→    has_complex: bool,\n   273→):\n   274→    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   275→        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   276→\n   277→    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n   278→        grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]\n   279→        exp_avg = exp_avgs[i]\n   280→        exp_avg_sq = exp_avg_sqs[i]\n   281→        step_t = state_steps[i]\n   282→\n   283→        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   284→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   285→            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n   286→            assert (\n   287→                param.device.type == step_t.device.type\n   288→                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   289→            ), (\n   290→                f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   291→            )\n   292→\n   293→        if torch.is_complex(param):\n   294→            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n   295→            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n   296→            exp_avg = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg)\n   297→            exp_avg_sq = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg_sq)\n   298→\n   299→        # update step\n   300→        step_t += 1\n   301→        step = step_t if capturable else _get_value(step_t)\n   302→\n   303→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   304→            if decoupled_weight_decay:\n   305→                param.mul_(1 - lr * weight_decay)\n   306→            else:\n   307→                grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   308→\n   309→        # Decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n   310→        exp_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - beta1)\n   311→        exp_avg_sq.mul_(beta2).addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1 - beta2)\n   312→\n   313→        bias_correction1 = 1 - beta1**step\n   314→        bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n   315→\n   316→        # correcting bias for the first moving moment\n   317→        bias_corrected_exp_avg = exp_avg / bias_correction1\n   318→\n   319→        # maximum length of the approximated SMA\n   320→        rho_inf = 2 / (1 - beta2) - 1\n   321→        # compute the length of the approximated SMA\n   322→        rho_t = rho_inf - 2 * step * (beta2**step) / bias_correction2\n   323→\n   324→        def _compute_rect():\n   325→            return (\n   326→                (rho_t - 4)\n   327→                * (rho_t - 2)\n   328→                * rho_inf\n   329→                / ((rho_inf - 4) * (rho_inf - 2) * rho_t)\n   330→            ) ** 0.5\n   331→\n   332→        def _compute_adaptive_lr():\n   333→            exp_avg_sq_sqrt = exp_avg_sq.sqrt()\n   334→            if differentiable:\n   335→                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = exp_avg_sq_sqrt.add(eps)\n   336→            else:\n   337→                exp_avg_sq_sqrt = exp_avg_sq_sqrt.add_(eps)\n   338→\n   339→            return (bias_correction2**0.5) / exp_avg_sq_sqrt\n   340→\n   341→        # Compute the variance rectification term and update parameters accordingly\n   342→        if capturable:\n   343→            update = torch.where(\n   344→                rho_t > 5.0, _compute_rect() * _compute_adaptive_lr(), 1.0\n   345→            )\n   346→            param.add_(bias_corrected_exp_avg * lr * update, alpha=-1.0)\n   347→        else:\n   348→            if rho_t > 5.0:\n   349→                param.add_(\n   350→                    bias_corrected_exp_avg\n   351→                    * lr\n   352→                    * _compute_adaptive_lr()\n   353→                    * _compute_rect(),\n   354→                    alpha=-1.0,\n   355→                )\n   356→            else:\n   357→                param.add_(bias_corrected_exp_avg * lr, alpha=-1.0)\n   358→\n   359→\n   360→def _multi_tensor_radam(\n   361→    params: list[Tensor],\n   362→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   363→    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   364→    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   365→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   366→    *,\n   367→    beta1: float,\n   368→    beta2: float,\n   369→    lr: float,\n   370→    weight_decay: float,\n   371→    eps: float,\n   372→    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n   373→    differentiable: bool,\n   374→    maximize: bool,\n   375→    capturable: bool,\n   376→    has_complex: bool,\n   377→):\n   378→    if len(params) == 0:\n   379→        return\n   380→\n   381→    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n   382→\n   383→    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   384→    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   385→        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n   386→            supports_xla=False\n   387→        )\n   388→        assert all(\n   389→            p.device.type == step.device.type\n   390→            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   391→            for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n   392→        ), (\n   393→            f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   394→        )\n   395→\n   396→    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   397→\n   398→    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n   399→        [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_avg_sqs, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n   400→    )\n   401→    for (\n   402→        grouped_params_,\n   403→        grouped_grads_,\n   404→        grouped_exp_avgs_,\n   405→        grouped_exp_avg_sqs_,\n   406→        grouped_state_steps_,\n   407→    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n   408→        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n   409→        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n   410→        grouped_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_avgs_)\n   411→        grouped_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_avg_sqs_)\n   412→        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n   413→\n   414→        # Update steps\n   415→        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   416→        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   417→        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   418→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   419→            torch._foreach_add_(\n   420→                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   421→            )\n   422→        else:\n   423→            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n   424→\n   425→        if has_complex:\n   426→            _view_as_real(\n   427→                grouped_params, grouped_grads, grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_exp_avg_sqs\n   428→            )\n   429→\n   430→        if maximize:\n   431→            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   432→\n   433→        # maximum length of the approximated SMA\n   434→        rho_inf = 2 / (1 - beta2) - 1\n   435→        # compute the length of the approximated SMA\n   436→        bias_correction1: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   437→        bias_correction2: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   438→        rho_t_list: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   439→        if capturable:\n   440→            bias_correction1 = torch._foreach_pow(beta2, grouped_state_steps)\n   441→            torch._foreach_neg_(bias_correction1)\n   442→            torch._foreach_add_(bias_correction1, 1)\n   443→            bias_correction2 = torch._foreach_pow(beta2, grouped_state_steps)\n   444→            torch._foreach_mul_(bias_correction2, grouped_state_steps)\n   445→            torch._foreach_mul_(bias_correction2, 2)\n   446→            torch._foreach_div_(bias_correction2, bias_correction1)\n   447→            torch._foreach_neg_(bias_correction2)\n   448→            torch._foreach_add_(bias_correction2, rho_inf)\n   449→            rho_t_list = bias_correction2\n   450→        else:\n   451→            rho_t_list = [\n   452→                rho_inf\n   453→                - 2\n   454→                * _get_value(step)\n   455→                * (beta2 ** _get_value(step))\n   456→                / (1 - beta2 ** _get_value(step))\n   457→                for step - lr * weight_decay)\n   463→            else:\n   464→                # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) already allocated for maximize\n   465→                if maximize:\n   466→     grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   471→                        grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n   472→                    )\n   473→\n   474→        # Decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n   475→        torch._foreach_lerp_(grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_grads, 1 - beta1)\n   476→\n   477→        torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_exp_avg_sqs, beta2)\n   478→        torch._foreach_addcmul_(\n   479→            grouped_exp_avg_sqs, grouped_grads, grouped_grads, 1 - beta2\n   480→        )\n   481→\n   482→        # Delete the local intermediate since it won't be used anymore to save on peak memory\n   483→        del grouped_grads\n   484→\n   485→        if capturable:\n   486→            num = torch._foreach_sub(rho_t_list, 4)\n   487→            sub2 = torch._foreach_sub(rho_t_list, 2)\n   488→            torch._foreach_mul_(num, sub2)\n   489→            del sub2\n   490→            torch._foreach_mul_(num, rho_inf)\n   491→            rho_inf = (rho_inf - 4) * (rho_inf - 2)\n   492→            denom = torch._foreach_mul(rho_t_list, rho_inf)\n   493→            torch._foreach_div_(num, denom)\n   494→            del denom\n   495→            torch._foreach_sqrt_(num)\n   496→\n   497→            # TODO(mlazos): we should try and get a foreach_where op https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/117884\n   498→            rect = [\n   499→                torch.where(rho_t > 5.0, n, 0.0) for n, rho_t in zip(num, rho_t_list)\n   500→            ]\n   501→            del num\n   502→            del rho_t_list\n   503→            unrect_step_size = [torch.where(rect > 0, 0.0, 1.0) for rect in rect]\n   504→            torch._foreach_mul_(unrect_step_size, lr)\n   505→\n   506→            bias_correction1 = torch._foreach_pow(beta1, grouped_state_steps)\n   507→            torch._foreach_neg_(bias_correction1)\n   508→            torch._foreach_add_(bias_correction1, 1)\n   509→\n   510→            torch._foreach_div_(unrect_step_size, bias_correction1)\n   511→            torch._foreach_neg_(unrect_step_size)\n   512→\n   513→            bias_correction2 = torch._foreach_pow(beta2, grouped_state_steps)\n   514→            torch._foreach_neg_(bias_correction2)\n   515→            torch._foreach_add_(bias_correction2, 1)\n   516→            torch._foreach_sqrt_(bias_correction2)\n   517→            torch._foreach_mul_(bias_correction2, lr)\n   518→            torch._foreach_mul_(bias_correction2, rect)\n   519→            del rect\n   520→            torch._foreach_neg_(bias_correction2)\n   521→            torch._foreach_div_(bias_correction2, bias_correction1)\n   522→            del bias_correction1\n   523→        else:\n   524→            rect = [\n   525→                (  # type: ignore[misc]\n   526→                    (rho_t - 4)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n   527→                    * (rho_t - 2)\n   528→                    * rho_inf\n   529→                    / ((rho_inf - 4) * (rho_inf - 2) * rho_t)\n   530→                )\n   531→                ** 0.5\n   532→                if rho_t > 5\n   533→                else 0\n   534→                for rho_t in rho_t_list\n   535→            ]\n   536→            unrectified = [0 if rect > 0 else 1.0 for rect in rect]\n   537→\n   538→            bias_correction1 = [\n   539→                1 - beta1 ** _get_value(step) for step in grouped_state_steps\n   540→            ]\n   541→            unrect_step_size = [\n   542→                (lr * rect / bc) * -1 for rect, bc in zip(unrectified, bias_correction1)\n   543→            ]\n   544→            bias_correction2 = [\n   545→                ((1 - beta2 ** _get_value(step)) ** 0.5) * (lr * rect / bc) * -1\n   546→                for step, rect, bc in zip(grouped_state_steps, rect, bias_correction1)\n   547→            ]\n   548→\n   549→        buffer = torch._foreach_sqrt(grouped_exp_avg_sqs)\n   550→        torch._foreach_add_(buffer, eps)\n   551→        torch._foreach_div_(buffer, bias_correction2)\n   552→        torch._foreach_reciprocal_(buffer)\n   553→        torch._foreach_add_(buffer, unrect_step_size)\n   554→\n   555→        # Here, buffer = sqrt(1 - beta2^t) * rect_step_size / (sqrt(v) + eps) + unrect_step_size\n   556→        torch._foreach_addcmul_(grouped_params, grouped_exp_avgs, buffer)\n   557→\n   558→\n   559→@_disable_dynamo_if_unsupported(single_tensor_fn=_single_tensor_radam)\n   560→def radam(\n   561→    params: list[Tensor],\n   562→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   563→    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   564→    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   565→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   566→    # kwonly args with defaults are not supported by functions compiled with torchscript issue #70627\n   567→    # setting this as kwarg for now as functional API is compiled by torch/distributed/optim\n   568→    decoupled_weight_decay: bool = False,\n   569→    foreach: Optional[bool] = None,\n   570→    differentiable: bool = False,\n   571→    capturable: bool = False,\n   572→    has_complex: bool = False,\n   573→    maximize: bool = False,\n   574→    *,\n   575→    beta1: float,\n   576→    beta2: float,\n   577→    lr: float,\n   578→    weight_decay: float,\n   579→    eps: float,\n   580→):\n   581→    r\"\"\"Functional API that performs RAdam algorithm computation.\n   582→\n   583→    See :class:`~torch.optim.RAdam` for details.\n   584→    \"\"\"\n   585→    if not all(isinstance(t, torch.Tensor) for t in state_steps):\n   586→        raise RuntimeError(\n   587→            \"API has changed, `state_steps` argument must contain a list of singleton tensors\"\n   588→        )\n   589→\n   590→    if foreach is None:\n   591→        _, foreach = RuntimeError(\"torch.jit.script not supported with foreach optimizers\")\n   597→\n   598→    if foreach and not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   599→        func = _multi_tensor_radam\n   600→ torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   340→\n   341→        # Update steps\n   342→        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   343→        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   344→        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   345→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   346→            torch._foreach_add_(\n   347→                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   348→            )\n   349→        else:\n   350→            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n   351→\n   352→        # update param\n   370→        # param * (1 - lambd * eta) - eta * grad\n   371→        # => param - param * lambd * eta - eta * grad\n   372→        # => param - eta * intermediate\n   373→        torch._foreach_addcmul_(grouped_params, intermediate, grouped_etas, value=-1)\n   374→        del intermediate\n   375→\n   376→        # we use the mus in other places)\n   381→        # all dtypes need to match, so we could introduce a cast in a loop\n   382→        # but since this only adds one additional kernel launch, this looks like the cleaner\n   383→        # and faster solution\n   384→        intermediate = torch._foreach_sub(grouped_params, grouped_axs)\n   385→        torch._foreach_addcmul_(grouped_axs, intermediate, grouped_mus)\n   386→        del intermediate\n   387→\n   388→        new_etas: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   389→        new_mus: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   390→        if capturable:\n   391→            # torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"step\"]):\n    88→                        step_val = float(p_state[\"step\"])\n    89→                        p_state[\"step\"] = (\n    90→                            torch.tensor(\n    91→                                step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device\n    92→                            )\n    93→                            if group[\"capturable\"]\n    94→                            else torch.tensor(step_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n    95→                        )\n    96→                    if not torch.is_tensor(p_state[\"mu_product\"]):\n    97→                        mu_prod_val = p_state[\"mu_product\"]\n    98→                        p_state[\"mu_product\"] = (\n    99→                            torch.tensor(\n   100→                                mu_prod_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device\n   101→                            )\n   102→                            if group[\"capturable\"]\n   103→                            else torch.tensor(mu_prod_val, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n   104→                        )\n   105→\n   106→    def _init_group(\n   107→        self,\n   108→        group,\n   109→        params_with_grad,\n   110→        grads,\n   111→        exp_avgs,\n   112→        exp_avg_sqs,\n   113→        mu_products,\n   114→        state_steps,\n   115→    ):\n   116→        has_complex = False\n   117→        for p in group[\"params\"]:\n   118→            if p.grad is not None:\n   119→                has_complex |= torch.is_complex(p)\n   120→                params_with_grad.append(p)\n   121→                if p.grad.is_sparse:\n   122→                    raise RuntimeError(\"NAdam does not support sparse gradients\")\n   123→                grads.append(p.grad)\n   124→\n   125→                state = self.state[p]\n   126→                # Lazy state initialization\n   127→                if len(state) == 0:\n   128→                    # note(crcrpar): [special device hosting for step]\n   129→                    # Deliberately host `step` and `mu_product` on CPU if capturable is False.\n   130→                    # This is because kernel launches are costly on CUDA and XLA.\n   131→                    state[\"step\"] = (\n   132→                        torch.zeros((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device)\n   133→                        if group[\"capturable\"]\n   134→                        else torch.tensor(0.0, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n   135→                    )\n   136→                    state[\"mu_product\"] = (\n   137→                        torch.ones((), dtype=_get_scalar_dtype(), device=p.device)\n   138→                        if group[\"capturable\"]\n   139→                        else torch.tensor(1.0, dtype=_get_scalar_dtype())\n   140→                    )\n   141→                    # Exponential moving average of gradient values\n   142→                    state[\"exp_avg\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n   143→                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n   144→                    )\n   145→                    # Exponential moving average of squared gradient values\n   146→                    state[\"exp_avg_sq\"] = torch.zeros_like(\n   147→                        p, memory_format=torch.preserve_format\n   148→                    )\n   149→\n   150→                exp_avgs.append(state[\"exp_avg\"])\n   151→                exp_avg_sqs.append(state[\"exp_avg_sq\"])\n   152→                mu_products.append(state[\"mu_product\"])\n   153→                state_steps.append(state[\"step\"])\n   154→        return has_complex\n   155→\n   156→    @_use_grad_for_differentiable\n   157→    def step(self, closure=None):\n   158→        \"\"\"Perform a single optimization step.\n   159→\n   160→        Args:\n   161→            closure (Callable, optional): A closure that reevaluates the model\n   162→                and returns the loss.\n   163→        \"\"\"\n   164→        self._cuda_graph_capture_health_check()\n   165→\n   166→        loss = None\n   167→        if closure is not None:\n   168→            with torch.enable_grad():\n   169→                loss = closure()\n   170→\n   171→        for group in self.param_groups:\n   172→            params_with_grad: list[Tensor] = []\n   173→            grads: list[Tensor] = []\n   174→            exp_avgs: list[Tensor] = []\n   175→            exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor] = []\n   176→            mu_products: list[Tensor] = []\n   177→            state_steps: list[Tensor] = []\n   178→            beta1, beta2 = cast(tuple[float, float], group[\"betas\"])\n   179→\n   180→            has_complex = self._init_group(\n   181→                group,\n   182→                params_with_grad,\n   183→                grads,\n   184→                exp_avgs,\n   185→                exp_avg_sqs,\n   186→                mu_products,\n   187→                state_steps,\n   188→            )\n   189→\n   190→            nadam(\n   191→                params_with_grad,\n   192→                grads,\n   193→                exp_avgs,\n   194→                exp_avg_sqs,\n   195→                mu_products,\n   196→                state_steps,\n   197→                beta1=beta1,\n   198→                beta2=beta2,\n   199→                lr=group[\"lr\"],\n   200→                weight_decay=group[\"weight_decay\"],\n   201→                momentum_decay=group[\"momentum_decay\"],\n   202→                eps=group[\"eps\"],\n   203→                maximize=group[\"maximize\"],\n   204→                decoupled_weight_decay=group[\"decoupled_weight_decay\"],\n   205→                foreach=group[\"foreach\"],\n   206→                capturable=group[\"capturable\"],\n   207→                differentiable=group[\"differentiable\"],\n   208→                has_complex=has_complex,\n   209→            )\n   210→\n   211→        return loss\n   212→\n   213→\n   214→NAdam.__doc__ = (\n   215→    r\"\"\"Implements NAdam algorithm.\n   216→\n   217→    .. math::\n   218→       \\begin{aligned}\n   219→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   220→            &\\textbf{input}      : \\gamma_t \\text{ (lr)}, \\: \\beta_1,\\beta_2 \\text{ (betas)},\n   221→                \\: \\theta_0 \\text{ (params)}, \\: f(\\theta) \\text{ (objective)}                   \\\\\n   222→            &\\hspace{13mm} \\: \\lambda \\text{ (weight decay)}, \\:\\psi \\text{ (momentum decay)}    \\\\\n   223→            &\\hspace{13mm} \\: \\textit{decoupled\\_weight\\_decay}, \\:\\textit{maximize}             \\\\\n   224→            &\\textbf{initialize} :  m_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( first moment)},\n   225→                v_0 \\leftarrow 0 \\text{ ( second moment)}                                 \\\\[-1.ex]\n   226→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                                 \\\\\n   227→            &\\textbf{for} \\: t=1 \\: \\textbf{to} \\: \\ldots \\: \\textbf{do}                         \\\\\n   228→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{maximize}:                                       \\\\\n   229→            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   -\\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})         \\\\\n   230→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\textbf{else}                                                           \\\\\n   231→            &\\hspace{10mm}g_t           \\leftarrow   \\nabla_{\\theta} f_t (\\theta_{t-1})          \\\\\n   232→            &\\hspace{5mm} \\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1}                                       \\\\\n   233→            &\\hspace{5mm} \\textbf{if} \\: \\lambda \\neq 0                                          \\\\\n   234→            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{if} \\: \\textit{decoupled\\_weight\\_decay}                       \\\\\n   235→            &\\hspace{15mm} \\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_{t-1} - \\gamma \\lambda \\theta_{t-1}                    \\\\\n   236→            &\\hspace{10mm}\\textbf{else}                                                          \\\\\n   237→            &\\hspace{15mm} g_t \\leftarrow g_t + \\lambda \\theta_{t-1}                             \\\\\n   238→            &\\hspace{5mm} \\mu_t \\leftarrow \\beta_1 \\big(1 - \\frac{1}{2}  0.96^{t \\psi} \\big)     \\\\\n   239→            &\\hspace{5mm} \\mu_{t+1} \\leftarrow \\beta_1 \\big(1 - \\frac{1}{2} 0.96^{(t+1)\\psi}\\big)\\\\\n   240→            &\\hspace{5mm}m_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_1 m_{t-1} + (1 - \\beta_1) g_t          \\\\\n   241→            &\\hspace{5mm}v_t           \\leftarrow   \\beta_2 v_{t-1} + (1-\\beta_2) g^2_t          \\\\\n   242→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\widehat{m_t} \\leftarrow \\mu_{t+1} m_t/(1-\\prod_{i=1}^{t+1}\\mu_i)\\\\[-1.ex]\n   243→            & \\hspace{11mm} + (1-\\mu_t) g_t /(1-\\prod_{i=1}^{t} \\mu_{i})                         \\\\\n   244→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\widehat{v_t} \\leftarrow   v_t/\\big(1-\\beta_2^t \\big)                   \\\\\n   245→            &\\hspace{5mm}\\theta_t \\leftarrow \\theta_t - \\gamma \\widehat{m_t}/\n   246→                \\big(\\sqrt{\\widehat{v_t}} + \\epsilon \\big)                                       \\\\\n   247→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   248→            &\\bf{return} \\:  \\theta_t                                                     \\\\[-1.ex]\n   249→            &\\rule{110mm}{0.4pt}                                                          \\\\[-1.ex]\n   250→       \\end{aligned}\n   251→\n   252→    For further details regarding the algorithm we refer to `Incorporating Nesterov Momentum into Adam`_.\n   253→    \"\"\"\n   254→    + rf\"\"\"\n   255→    Args:\n   256→        {_params_doc}\n   257→        lr (float, Tensor, optional): learning rate (default: 2e-3)\n   258→        betas (Tuple[float, float], optional): coefficients used for computing\n   259→            running averages of gradient and its square (default: (0.9, 0.999))\n   260→        eps (float, optional): term added to the denominator to improve\n   261→            numerical stability (default: 1e-8)\n   262→        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay (L2 penalty) (default: 0)\n   263→        momentum_decay (float, optional): momentum momentum_decay (default: 4e-3)\n   264→        decoupled_weight_decay (bool, optional): whether to decouple the weight\n   265→            decay as in AdamW to obtain NAdamW. If True, the algorithm does not\n   266→            accumulate weight decay in the momentum nor variance. (default: False)\n   267→        {_foreach_doc}\n   268→        {_maximize_doc}\n   269→        {_capturable_doc}\n   270→        {_differentiable_doc}\n   271→\n   272→    .. _Incorporating Nesterov Momentum into Adam:\n   273→        https://openreview.net/forum?id=OM0jvwB8jIp57ZJjtNEZ\n   274→    .. _Decoupled Weight Decay Regularization:\n   275→        https://arxiv.org/abs/1711.05101\n   276→\n   277→    \"\"\"\n   278→)\n   279→\n   280→\n   281→def _single_tensor_nadam(\n   282→    params: list[Tensor],\n   283→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   284→    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   285→    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   286→    mu_products: list[Tensor],\n   287→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   288→    *,\n   289→    beta1: float,\n   290→    beta2: float,\n   291→    lr: float,\n   292→    weight_decay: float,\n   293→    momentum_decay: float,\n   294→    eps: float,\n   295→    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n   296→    maximize: bool,\n   297→    capturable: bool,\n   298→    differentiable: bool,\n   299→    has_complex: bool,\n   300→):\n   301→    if not torch.jit.is_scripting():\n   302→        lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   303→\n   304→    for i, param in enumerate(params):\n   305→        grad = grads[i] if not maximize else -grads[i]\n   306→        exp_avg = exp_avgs[i]\n   307→        exp_avg_sq = exp_avg_sqs[i]\n   308→        mu_product = mu_products[i]\n   309→        step_t = state_steps[i]\n   310→\n   311→        if torch.is_complex(param):\n   312→            param = torch.view_as_real(param)\n   313→            grad = torch.view_as_real(grad)\n   314→            exp_avg = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg)\n   315→            exp_avg_sq = torch.view_as_real(exp_avg_sq)\n   316→\n   317→        # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   318→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   319→            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices()\n   320→            assert (\n   321→                param.device.type == mu_product.device.type == step_t.device.type\n   322→                and param.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   323→            ), (\n   324→                f\"If capturable=True, params, mu_products and state_steps must be \"\n   325→                f\"on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   326→            )\n   327→\n   328→        # update step\n   329→        step_t += 1\n   330→\n   331→        if capturable:\n   332→            step = step_t\n   333→        else:\n   334→            step = _get_value(step_t)\n   335→\n   336→        bias_correction2 = 1 - beta2**step\n   337→\n   338→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   339→            if decoupled_weight_decay:\n   340→                # Perform stepweight decay\n   341→                param.mul_(1 - lr * weight_decay)\n   342→            else:\n   343→                grad = grad.add(param, alpha=weight_decay)\n   344→\n   345→        # calculate the momentum cache \\mu^{t} and \\mu^{t+1}\n   346→        mu = beta1 * (1.0 - 0.5 * (0.96 ** (step * momentum_decay)))\n   347→        mu_next = beta1 * (1.0 - 0.5 * (0.96 ** ((step + 1) * momentum_decay)))\n   348→\n   349→        # update mu_product\n   350→        mu_product *= mu\n   351→\n   352→        # decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n   353→        exp_avg.lerp_(grad, 1 - beta1)\n   354→        exp_avg_sq.mul_(beta2).addcmul_(grad, grad, value=1 - beta2)\n   355→        denom = exp_avg_sq.div(bias_correction2).sqrt()\n   356→\n   357→        if differentiable or capturable:\n   358→            denom = denom.add(eps)\n   359→            # Make autograd track the operations\n   360→            # by updating the grad and exp_avg directly and not using the\n   361→            # scalar \"value\" argument of addcdiv.\n   362→            mu_product_next = mu_product * mu_next\n   363→            grad = grad * (-lr * (1.0 - mu) / (1.0 - mu_product))\n   364→            exp_avg = exp_avg * (-lr * mu_next / (1.0 - mu_product_next))\n   365→            param.addcdiv_(grad, denom)\n   366→            param.addcdiv_(exp_avg, denom)\n   367→        else:\n   368→            mu_product_next = _get_value(mu_product) * mu_next\n   369→            denom.add_(eps)\n   370→            param.addcdiv_(\n   371→                grad, denom, value=(-lr * (1.0 - mu) / (1.0 - _get_value(mu_product)))\n   372→            )\n   373→            param.addcdiv_(\n   374→                exp_avg,\n   375→                denom,\n   376→                value=cast(float, (-lr * mu_next) / (1.0 - mu_product_next)),\n   377→            )\n   378→\n   379→\n   380→def _multi_tensor_nadam(\n   381→    params: list[Tensor],\n   382→    grads: list[Tensor],\n   383→    exp_avgs: list[Tensor],\n   384→    exp_avg_sqs: list[Tensor],\n   385→    mu_products: list[Tensor],\n   386→    state_steps: list[Tensor],\n   387→    *,\n   388→    beta1: float,\n   389→    beta2: float,\n   390→    lr: float,\n   391→    weight_decay: float,\n   392→    momentum_decay: float,\n   393→    eps: float,\n   394→    decoupled_weight_decay: bool,\n   395→    maximize: bool,\n   396→    capturable: bool,\n   397→    differentiable: bool,\n   398→    has_complex: bool,\n   399→):\n   400→    if len(params) == 0:\n   401→        return\n   402→\n   403→    assert not differentiable, \"_foreach ops don't support autograd\"\n   404→\n   405→    # If compiling, the compiler will handle cudagraph checks, see note [torch.compile x capturable]\n   406→    if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n   407→        capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n   408→            supports_xla=False\n   409→        )\n   410→        assert all(\n   411→            p.device.type == mp.device.type == step.device.type\n   412→            and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n   413→            for p, mp, step in zip(params, mu_products, state_steps)\n   414→        ), (\n   415→            \"If capturable=True, \"\n   416→            \"params, mu_products, and state_steps must be on supported devices: \"\n   417→            f\"{capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n   418→        )\n   419→\n   420→    lr = _to_scalar(lr)\n   421→\n   422→    grouped_tensors = Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n   423→        [params, grads, exp_avgs, exp_avg_sqs, mu_products, state_steps]  # type: ignore[list-item]\n   424→    )\n   425→    for (\n   426→        grouped_params_,\n   427→        grouped_grads_,\n   428→        grouped_exp_avgs_,\n   429→        grouped_exp_avg_sqs_,\n   430→        grouped_mu_products_,\n   431→        grouped_state_steps_,\n   432→    ), _ in grouped_tensors.values():\n   433→        grouped_params = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_params_)\n   434→        grouped_grads = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_grads_)\n   435→        grouped_exp_avgs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_avgs_)\n   436→        grouped_exp_avg_sqs = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_exp_avg_sqs_)\n   437→        grouped_mu_products = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_mu_products_)\n   438→        grouped_state_steps = cast(list[Tensor], grouped_state_steps_)\n   439→\n   440→        # handle complex\n   441→        if has_complex:\n   442→            _view_as_real(\n   443→                grouped_params, grouped_grads, grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_exp_avg_sqs\n   444→            )\n   445→\n   446→        if maximize:\n   447→            grouped_grads = torch._foreach_neg(grouped_grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   448→\n   449→        # Update steps\n   450→        # If steps are on CPU, foreach will fall back to the slow path, which is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   451→        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   452→        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   453→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   454→            torch._foreach_add_(\n   455→                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   456→            )\n   457→        else:\n   458→            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n   459→\n   460→        if weight_decay != 0:\n   461→            if decoupled_weight_decay:\n   462→                # Perform stepweight decay\n   463→                torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_params, 1 - lr * weight_decay)\n   464→            else:\n   465→                # Reuse the intermediate memory (grouped_grads) already allocated for maximize\n   466→                if maximize:\n   467→                    torch._foreach_add_(\n   468→                        grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n   469→                    )\n   470→                else:\n   471→                    grouped_grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n   472→                        grouped_grads, grouped_params, alpha=weight_decay\n   473→                    )\n   474→\n   475→        # Decay the first and second moment running average coefficient\n   476→        torch._foreach_lerp_(grouped_exp_avgs, grouped_grads, 1 - beta1)\n   477→\n   478→        torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_exp_avg_sqs, beta2)\n   479→        torch._foreach_addcmul_(\n   480→            grouped_exp_avg_sqs, grouped_grads, grouped_grads, 1 - beta2\n   481→        )\n   482→\n   483→        exp_avg_sq_sqrt = torch._foreach_sqrt(grouped_exp_avg_sqs)\n   484→\n   485→        bias_correction_sqrt: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   486→        mus: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   487→        mu_nexts: Union[tuple[Tensor, ...], list[Tensor]]\n   488→        if capturable:\n   489→            # mus will be beta1 * (1 - 0.5 * 0.96 ** (step * momentum_decay))\n   490→            exponent = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_state_steps, momentum_decay)\n   491→            mus = torch._foreach_pow(0.96, exponent)\n   492→            torch._foreach_mul_(mus, -0.5)\n   493→            torch._foreach_add_(mus, 1.0)\n   494→            torch._foreach_mul_(mus, beta1)\n   495→\n   496→            # mu_nexts will be beta1 * (1 - 0.5 * 0.96 ** ((step + 1) * momentum_decay))\n   497→            torch._foreach_add_(exponent, momentum_decay)\n   498→            mu_nexts = torch._foreach_pow(0.96, exponent)\n   499→            torch._foreach_mul_(mu_nexts, -0.5)\n   500→            torch._foreach_add_(mu_nexts, 1.0)\n   501→            torch._foreach_mul_(mu_nexts, beta1)\n   502→\n   503→            # save peak memory as we don't need exponent anymore\n   504→            del exponent\n   505→\n   506→            bias_correction_sqrt = torch._foreach_pow(beta2, grouped_state_steps)\n   507→            # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n   508→            torch._foreach_sub_(bias_correction_sqrt, 1.0)\n   509→            torch._foreach_neg_(bias_correction_sqrt)\n   510→            torch._foreach_sqrt_(bias_correction_sqrt)\n   511→        else:\n   512→            bias_correction_sqrt = [\n   513→                (1 - beta2 ** _get_value(step)) ** 0.5 for step in grouped_state_steps\n   514→            ]\n   515→            mus = [\n   516→                beta1 * (1.0 - 0.5 * (0.96 ** (_get_value(step) * momentum_decay)))\n   517→                for step in grouped_state_steps\n   518→            ]\n   519→            mu_nexts = [\n   520→                beta1\n   521→                * (1.0 - 0.5 * (0.96 ** ((_get_value(step) + 1) * momentum_decay)))\n   522→                for step in grouped_state_steps\n   523→            ]\n   524→\n   525→        # update mu_products\n   526→        torch._foreach_mul_(grouped_mu_products, mus)\n   527→\n   528→        torch._foreach_div_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, bias_correction_sqrt)\n   529→        torch._foreach_add_(exp_avg_sq_sqrt, eps)\n   530→\n   531→        # explicitly delete bias_correction refs to save memory\n   532→        del bias_correction_sqrt\n   533→\n   534→        if capturable:\n   535→            # Build up the step_size multiplier for grad, reusing mus' memory\n   536→            torch._foreach_sub_(mus, 1.0)\n   537→            torch._foreach_mul_(mus, lr)\n   538→            # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg\n   539→            denom = torch._foreach_sub(grouped_mu_products, 1.0)\n   540→            torch._foreach_neg_(denom)\n   541→            torch._foreach_div_(mus, denom)\n   542→            # - lr * (1 - mu) / (1 - mu_product)\n   543→            step_size_grads = mus\n   544→            # explicitly delete denom to save memory\n   545→            del denom\n   546→\n   547→            # Build up the step_size multiplier for exp_avg, reusing mu_nexts' memory\n   548→            denom = torch._foreach_mul(grouped_mu_products, mu_nexts)\n   549→            torch._foreach_mul_(mu_nexts, lr)\n   550→            # foreach_sub doesn't allow a scalar as the first arg, but it's okay because\n   551→            # we need a negative here anyway\n   552→            torch._foreach_sub_(denom, 1.0)\n   553→            torch._foreach_div_(mu_nexts, denom)\n   554→            # - lr * mu_next / (1 - mu_product * mu_next)\n   555→            step_size_expavg = mu_nexts\n   556→            # explicitly delete denom to save memory\n   557→            del denom\n   558→\n   559→            # we cannot inplace into step_size_grads cuz it is a list of ScalarTensors\n   560→            # and mul'ing with grouped_grads will result in a list of bigger Tensors\n   561→            numerator = torch._foreach_mul(step_size_grads, grouped_grads)\n   562→            torch._foreach_addcmul_(numerator, step_size_expavg, grouped_exp_avgs)\n   563→\n   564→            # finally, update params\n   565→            torch._foreach_addcdiv_(grouped_params, numerator, exp_avg_sq_sqrt)\n   566→        else:\n   567→ mu_product, mu in zip(grouped_mu_products, mus)\n   571→                ]\n   572→            )\n   573→            step_size_expavg = _stack_if_compiling(\n   574→                [\n   575→ Rates with Sublinear Memory Cost`_.\n   225→    \"\"\"\n   226→    + rf\"\"\"\n   227→    Args:\n   228→        {_params_doc}\n   229→        lr (float, Tensor, optional): unlike other optimizers, Adafactor does not require a\n   230→            learning rate, and Noam Shazeer and Mitchell Stern do not use lr at all.\n   231→            Deviating from the paper, this implementation uses lr for applying weight\n   232→            decay and as the maximum value for relative step size rho_t. Note that in\n   233→            the paper, a constant of 0.01 is used as the maximum value for relative\n   234→            step size, and so we set 0.01 as the default value. (default: 1e-2)\n   235→        beta2_decay (float, optional): the decay rate of beta2. beta2 standardly refers\n   236→            to the coefficient used for computing the running average of the gradient\n   237→            squared. (default: -0.8)\n   238→        eps (Tuple[float, float], optional): epsilon1 is the term added to the denominator\n   239→            of the update calculation to improve numerical stability. This use of epsilon1\n   240→            deviates from the algorithm written in the paper! See note below for more details.\n   241→            epsilon2 is the term used to avoid having too small a weight update when applying\n   242→            parameter scaling. (default: (None, 1e-3))\n   243→        d (float, optional): the clipping threshold, used to avoid larger-than-desired\n   244→            updates.\n   245→        weight_decay (float, optional): weight decay coefficient (default: 1e-2)\n   246→        foreach (bool, optional): whether foreach implementation of optimizer is used. Note\n   247→            that the foreach implementation uses ~ sizeof(params) more peak memory than the\n   248→            for-loop version due to the intermediates being a tensorlist vs just one tensor.\n   249→            As Adafactor is commonly used when memory is prohibitive, Adafactor will default\n   250→            to the slower single tensor for-loop implementation unless this flag is explicitly\n   251→            True. This behavior is contrary to other optimizers, which will attempt defaulting\n   252→            to foreach on CUDA for faster runtime. (default: None)\n   253→        {_maximize_doc}\"\"\"\n   254→    + r\"\"\"\n   255→    .. Note::\n   256→        The implementation of Adafactor subtly differs from Noam Shazeer and Mitchell Stern\n   257→        and implementations in some other frameworks with its use of learning rate and\n   258→        :math:`\\epsilon_1`.\n   259→\n   260→        Regarding the learning rate hyperparameter: Noam Shazeer and Mitchell Stern do not\n   261→        use lr at all, as the stated algorithm uses :math:`\\rho_t` and update clipping to\n   262→        affect the step size.\n   263→\n   264→        This implementation allows `lr` to influence the maximum value for :math:`\\rho_t`:\n   265→\n   266→        .. math::\n   267→            \\begin{aligned}\n   268→                &\\hspace{5mm}\\rho_t \\leftarrow min(lr, \\frac{1}{\\sqrt{t}})\n   269→            \\end{aligned}\n   270→\n   271→        This differs from Noam Shazeer and Mitchell Stern, who use a constant of 0.01 as\n   272→        the maximum value of :math:`\\rho_t`\n   273→\n   274→        .. math::\n   275→            \\begin{aligned}\n   276→                &\\hspace{5mm}\\rho_t \\leftarrow min(0.01, \\frac{1}{\\sqrt{t}})\n   277→            \\end{aligned}\n   278→\n   279→        Noam Shazeer and Mitchell Stern do not enforce an opinion on how weight decay should\n   280→        be computed, and so we use the learning rate as a coefficient for decoupled weight\n   281→        decay, similar to what is suggested in `Decoupled Weight Decay Regularization`_.\n   282→\n   283→        Regarding the use of :math:`\\epsilon_1`: The implementation attempts to replicate the\n   284→        presumed intention of Noam Shazeer and Mitchell Stern to use :math:`\\epsilon_1` as\n   285→        a stabilizing term when the squared gradient becomes small.\n   286→\n   287→        This stabilization can be written as\n   288→\n   289→        .. math::\n   290→            \\begin{aligned}\n   291→                &\\hspace{5mm}R_t \\leftarrow \\widehat{\\beta}_{2_t}R_{t-1}+\n   292→                    (1-\\widehat{\\beta}_{2_t})(G_t \\odot G_t + 1_n \\cdot 1^\\top_m) \\cdot 1_m          \\\\\n   293→                &\\hspace{5mm}C_t \\leftarrow \\widehat{\\beta}_{2_t}C_{t-1}+\n   294→                    (1-\\widehat{\\beta}_{2_t}) 1^\\top_n \\cdot (G_t \\odot G_t + 1_n \\cdot 1^\\top_m)    \\\\\n   295→                &\\hspace{5mm}\\widehat{V}_t \\leftarrow\n   296→                    \\frac{R_t \\cdot C_t}{max(1^\\top_n \\cdot R_t, \\epsilon_1)}                        \\\\\n   297→                &\\hspace{5mm}U_t \\leftarrow \\frac{G_t}{max(\\sqrt{\\widehat{V}_t}, \\epsilon_1)}        \\\\\n   298→            \\end{aligned}\n   299→\n   300→        where the row and column factors of gradient squared :math:`R_t` and :math:`C_t`\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. is a for-loop calling t.add(1) over\n   343→        # and over. 1 will then be wrapped into a Tensor over and over again, which is slower than if we just\n   344→        # wrapped it once now. The alpha is required to assure we go to the right overload.\n   345→        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and grouped_state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n   346→            torch._foreach_add_(\n   347→                grouped_state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n   348→            )\n   349→        else:\n   350→            torch._foreach_add_(grouped_state_steps, 1)\n   351→\n   352→        # float or Tensor value\n\n        Returns:\n            The value converted to scalar if it was a Tensor, otherwise the original value\n        \"\"\"\n        return _to_scalar(value)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n        tensor_lists: list[list[Optional[Tensor]]],\n        with_indices: bool = False,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Group tensors by device and dtype.\n\n        This is a wrapper around Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype\n        to make it easily accessible from the foreach utilities module.\n\n        Args:\n            tensor_lists: List of lists of tensors to group\n            with_indices: Whether to return indices for tracking\n\n        Returns:\n            Dictionary with (device, dtype) keys and grouped tensor lists as values\n        \"\"\"\n        return Optimizer._group_tensors_by_device_and_dtype(\n            tensor_lists, with_indices=with_indices\n        )\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def check_capturable_constraints(\n        params: list[Tensor],\n        state_steps: list[Tensor],\n        capturable: bool,\n        supports_xla: bool = False,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Check that parameters and state steps meet capturable mode requirements.\n\n        Args:\n            params: List of parameters\n            state_steps: List of state step tensors\n            capturable: Whether capturable mode is enabled\n            supports_xla: Whether to include XLA in supported devices\n\n        Raises:\n            AssertionError if capturable constraints are not met\n        \"\"\"\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and capturable:\n            capturable_supported_devices = _get_capturable_supported_devices(\n                supports_xla=supports_xla\n            )\n            assert all(\n                p.device.type == step.device.type\n                and p.device.type in capturable_supported_devices\n                for p, step in zip(params, state_steps)\n            ), (\n                f\"If capturable=True, params and state_steps must be on supported devices: {capturable_supported_devices}.\"\n            )\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def update_state_steps(\n        state_steps: list[Tensor],\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update state steps with optimization for CPU tensors.\n\n        When state_steps are on CPU, we wrap the increment value once instead of\n        wrapping it repeatedly in the foreach loop.\n\n        Args:\n            state_steps: List of state step tensors to increment\n        \"\"\"\n        if not torch.compiler.is_compiling() and state_steps and state_steps[0].is_cpu:\n            torch._foreach_add_(\n                state_steps, torch.tensor(1.0, device=\"cpu\"), alpha=1.0\n            )\n        else:\n            torch._foreach_add_(state_steps, 1)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def handle_maximize(\n        grads: list[Tensor],\n        params: list[Tensor],\n        weight_decay: float,\n        maximize: bool,\n    ) -> list[Tensor]:\n        \"\"\"Handle gradient negation for maximize mode and weight decay.\n\n        When maximize=True, gradients are negated. When weight_decay > 0 and maximize=True,\n        we can reuse the negated gradient tensor for weight decay computation.\n\n        Args:\n            grads: List of gradient tensors\n            params: List of parameter tensors\n            weight_decay: Weight decay coefficient\n            maximize: Whether to maximize loss\n\n        Returns:\n            Modified gradient list (may be the same list or a new list)\n        \"\"\"\n        if maximize:\n            grads = torch._foreach_neg(grads)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            # Reuse the intermediate memory (grads) already allocated for maximize\n            if maximize:\n                torch._foreach_add_(grads, params, alpha=weight_decay)\n            else:\n                grads = torch._foreach_add(  # type: ignore[assignment]\n                    grads, params, alpha=weight_decay\n                )\n\n        return grads\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def handle_decoupled_weight_decay(\n        params: list[Tensor],\n        lr: Union[float, Tensor],\n        weight_decay: float,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Handle decoupled weight decay (AdamW style).\n\n        In decoupled weight decay, the weight decay is applied directly to parameters\n        before the momentum/second moment updates, rather than being added to gradients.\n\n        Args:\n            params: List of parameter tensors\n            lr: Learning rate\n            weight_decay: Weight decay coefficient\n        \"\"\"\n        if weight_decay != 0:\n            torch._foreach_mul_(params, 1 - lr * weight_decay)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def handle_complex_dtype(\n        tensor_lists: list[list[Tensor]],\n        has_complex: bool,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Convert complex parameters to real view for foreach operations.\n\n        Complex tensors need special handling as most foreach operations don't support them.\n        This function converts them to a real view in-place.\n\n        Args:\n            tensor_lists: List of tensor lists to convert (e.g., [params, grads, exp_avgs, ...])\n            has_complex: Whether the parameter group contains complex parameters\n        \"\"\"\n        if has_complex:\n            _view_as_real(*tensor_lists)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def handle_grad_scale_and_found_inf(\n        grad_scale: {grad_scale.device: grad_scale} if grad_scale is not None else {}\n        )\n        found_inf_dict: DeviceDict = (\n            {found_inf.device: found_inf} if found_inf is not None else {}\n        )\n        return grad_scale_dict, found_inf_dict\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_device_grad_scale_and_found_inf(\n        device: torch.device,\n        grad_scale: Optional[Tensor],\n        found_inf: Optional[Tensor],\n        grad_scale_dict: DeviceDict,\n        found_inf_dict: DeviceDict,\n    ) -> tuple[Optional[Tensor], Optional[Tensor]]:\n        \"\"\"Get or create grad_scale and found_inf tensors for a specific device.\n\n        Args:\n            device: Target device\n            grad_scale: Original grad_scale tensor\n            found_inf: Original found_inf tensor\n            grad_scale_dict: Cache of grad_scale tensors by device\n            found_inf_dict: Cache of found_inf tensors by device\n\n        Returns:\n            Tuple of (device_grad_scale, device_found_inf)\n        \"\"\"\n        device_grad_scale = None\n        device_found_inf = None\n\n        if grad_scale is not None:\n            device_grad_scale = grad_scale_dict.setdefault(\n                device, grad_scale.to(device, non_blocking=True)\n            )\n        if found_inf is not None:\n            device_found_inf = found_inf_dict.setdefault(\n                device, found_inf.to(device, non_blocking=True)\n            )\n\n        return device_grad_scale, device_found_inf\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def check_sparse_grads(grads: list[Tensor], has_sparse_grad: bool) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if any gradients in the list are sparse.\n\n        Args:\n            grads: List of gradient tensors\n            has_sparse_grad: Whether the optimizer was initialized with sparse gradients\n\n        Returns:\n            True if any gradient is sparse, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        if has_sparse_grad:\n            return any(grad.is_sparse for grad in grads)\n        return False\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def get_device_param_tensor_dict(\n        params: list[Tensor],\n
